subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Solar Flares and Coronal Mass Ejections
description: A 60-second lesson on Solar Flares and Coronal Mass Ejections.

hook.text: Solar flares release bursts of radiation in minutes. Coronal mass ejections hurl billions of tons of plasma into space.

buildup.text: Both are caused by twisted magnetic field lines that snap and reconnect. The energy release is sudden and enormous.

discovery.text: The Carrington Event of 1859 was the strongest solar storm on record. It sparked auroras at the equator and fried telegraph wires.

twist.text: A Carrington-level event today could disable satellites, power grids, and communications worldwide for weeks or months.

climax.text: In May 2024, the strongest geomagnetic storm in 20 years hit Earth. Auroras were seen from places that rarely see them.

punchline.text: Solar tantrums reach Earth in hours. Our technology makes us more vulnerable than ever.

quiz.question: What would happen if a Carrington-level solar storm hit Earth today?
options: It could disable satellites, power grids, and communications worldwide for weeks
It would cool the Earth significantly by blocking sunlight
It would have no effect due to modern technology and shielding
